chief_complaint: A 45-year-old male presented with sudden vision loss in both eyes.
history: The patient had a 2-day history of severe abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting. He also had a history of hypertension and was on lisinopril. He denied any recent trauma or eye problems. He had a 10-pack-year smoking history and consumed 2-3 beers daily. He had a 10-year history of hypertension and was on lisinopril.
exam: Vital signs: BP 160/90 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20 bpm. Physical examination revealed abdominal tenderness and guarding. Fundoscopic examination showed Purtscher-like retinopathy in both eyes.
labs: Laboratory investigations: Elevated pancreatic enzymes (amylase 500 U/L, lipase 1000 U/L), elevated creatinine (2.5 mg/dL), proteinuria (3+ on dipstick), and hematuria (2+ on dipstick). Renal biopsy revealed membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis (MPGN).
correct_diagnosis: Acute pancreatitis with Purtscher-like retinopathy and membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis
differentials: Acute pancreatitis with Purtscher-like retinopathy and membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis
Acute kidney injury with membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis and Purtscher-like retinopathy
Acute pancreatitis with acute kidney injury and Purtscher-like retinopathy
Membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis with Purtscher-like retinopathy and acute pancreatitis
correct_ranking: Acute pancreatitis with Purtscher-like retinopathy and membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis
Acute kidney injury with membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis and Purtscher-like retinopathy
Membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis with Purtscher-like retinopathy and acute pancreatitis
